markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Expose backend APIs for retrieval explanation, bundle reproduction, stale reason, missing evidence, and repair actions. This MT belongs to RetrievalContextAndRanking. Retrieve project knowledge through explainable direct, graph, hybrid, and passage fallback modes.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
Retrieval must choose explicit modes: none, direct_load, exact_lookup, graph_traversal, hybrid_rag, or passage_fallback, with non_hybrid_reason where applicable.
Context bundles must be bounded, cited, explainable, restartable, and inspectable through backend and visual-debug surfaces.
scope.expected_behavior: RetrievalDebugApi leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: RetrievalDebugApi is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

evidence: Built+verified 387a43b2; RetrievalContextAndRanking: 46 unit + 10 real-PG tests pass; cheapest-authoritative planner records mode+skip-reason in replayable RetrievalTrace; reuses MT-060 trace/bundle substrate (1 new migration 0260); MT-129 additive QueryRetrievalMode extension; folded-stub bridges (139-142) preserve ProjectBrain/SemanticCatalog/AIReady/ContextPack intent as backend contracts.

evidence.finding: Repair-actions endpoint and dedicated stale/missing-evidence surfaces named by the contract were not implemented (explain/export/catalog only)
evidence.fix: GET bundles/:id/staleness (per-item ok|missing_evidence|source_stale + bundle stale flag + receipt) and POST bundles/:id/repair (re-executes the recorded query through the executed pipeline -> fresh linked bundle+trace, original retained); executor hardened to carry the required non_hybrid_reason on hybrid-origin passage fallbacks
evidence.tests: knowledge_retrieval_debug_api_tests 1 passed (mt143_staleness_surface_and_repair_action, real-PG route-level); knowledge_retrieval_pipeline_tests 13 passed regression

findings.title: repair-actions surface (and dedicated stale/missing-evidence endpoints) named by the contract are not implemented
findings.evidence: api/knowledge_retrieval.rs exposes explain_bundle + export_bundle_evidence + list_catalog (read-only). The contract names retrieval explanation, bundle reproduction, stale reason, missing evidence, and repair actions; there is no repair-action endpoint and no dedicated stale/missing-evidence route beyond what is embedded in the trace decisions JSON.
findings.fix: Add a repair-action endpoint (e.g. re-run/refresh a stale bundle, re-extract missing evidence) and/or surface stale/missing-evidence explicitly, or descope the contract.

claim_mismatches.claim: Expose backend APIs for retrieval explanation, bundle reproduction, stale reason, missing evidence, and repair actions
claim_mismatches.reality: explanation/reproduction/catalog are implemented; repair actions are not, and stale/missing evidence is only implicit in the trace

missing_tests: test: a retrieval repair action endpoint exists and records a receipt

wp_validator_review_v2.review_method: Integration Validator multi-agent workflow (wf_0ac25815-485 + wf_98386044-70a): independent per-MT reviewer + adversarial confirm on FAIL. No ACP. Governance/repo paperwork non-blocking per operator directive (2026-06-18). Bar = good code vs MT contract + WP packet + current Master Spec v02.194, plus the WP_VALIDATOR Spec-Realism Gate.
wp_validator_review_v2.summary: MT-143 requires backend APIs for retrieval explanation, bundle reproduction, stale-reason, missing-evidence, and repair actions with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority and real proof. The code delivers all five surfaces over real Axum routes with nav-identity receipts, and the proof is a real-PostgreSQL route-level test that exercises bundle creation, evidence deletion, missing_evidence detection, and repair re-execution.
findings: api/knowledge_retrieval.rs:71-91 wires 5 real Axum routes: GET bundles/:id (explain, returns bundle+items+replayable QueryPlan/RetrievalTrace), GET .../export, GET .../staleness, POST .../repair, GET catalog. This satisfies the MT scope: explanation + reproduction + stale reason + missing evidence + repair actions.

knowledge_retrieval.rs:147-214 every route requires backend-nav identity headers (actor/session/kernel-task-run/correlation) returning 400 otherwise, and appends a KnowledgeRetrievalTraceRecorded EventLedger receipt. This matches current Master Spec v02.194 02-system-architecture.md:2717 (backend nav calls MUST carry actor/session/correlation/target-ref/typed receipt; no wiki-scraping).

knowledge_retrieval.rs:309-419 staleness re-checks each bundle item's backing record against the live index (Span/Passage/Source/Claim -> ok|missing_evidence|source_stale; Entity intentionally ok with documented reason at :376-381) and sets bundle stale flag. Real per-item DB lookups via get_knowledge_span/get_knowledge_source/get_knowledge_memory_passage/get_knowledge_claim.

knowledge_retrieval.rs:429-499 repair re-executes the bundle's RECORDED query (extracted from trace decisions query_plan) through the real execute_retrieval pipeline, producing a fresh persisted bundle+trace linked old->new, retaining the original (append-only). Real executor call, not a stub.

executor.rs (commit 572bfe42) :158+ correctly populates non_hybrid_reason=NonHybridReason::BoundedExecutorContext on a hybrid-origin plan revised to PassageFallback, and copies it into the trace. This satisfies Master Spec v02.194 02-system-architecture.md:11528 (non-hybrid modes MUST populate non_hybrid_reason) and uses the spec-listed vocabulary value bounded_executor_context (spec:11416/11496). NonHybridReason::BoundedExecutorContext is a real enum variant (memory/retrieval_mode.rs:257).

tests/knowledge_retrieval_debug_api_tests.rs:151-306 mt143_staleness_surface_and_repair_action is a REAL-PG route-level test: spins the actual retrieval_api::routes Axum app against PostgreSQL (fixture header: 'Real PostgreSQL only. No SQLite, no mocks'), creates a real passage-backed bundle via execute_retrieval, asserts fresh=all-ok/stale=false, then deletes the passage row via raw SQL (:220-232) and asserts stale=true + a missing_evidence item, then POST repair returns a fresh persisted bundle_id != original while original is retained (:271-285), and asserts 400 (no headers) / 404 (ghost bundle). This exercises the Handshake-owned PostgreSQL/knowledge-store contract, not implementer mocks.

NoopRecorder/NoopLlmClient in the test are peripheral scaffolding for the flight-recorder and llm AppState fields, which the retrieval-debug routes do not use as the resource-under-proof; the durable authority path (knowledge store + postgres_pool + EventLedger receipts) is real. This does not constitute mock-only proof of the MT's claims.

Aside (non-blocking): MT contract still cites v02.192 anchors and lifecycle paperwork (claimed_by/completed_by null); resolved against current v02.194 per instructions, and paperwork drift is expected and not a failing reason.

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
